items.title: IRB extends tax filing deadline to May 15
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Taxpayers now have until May 15 to submit their Tax Return Form (BN) for the Year of Assessment 2025, following a 15-day extension granted by the Inland Revenue Board of Malaysia (IRB). IRB said the extension was given in line with the mandatory e-Filing system, allowing taxpayers more time to ensure accurate and complete submissions. "Taxpayers are advised to make full use of this extension by filing early to ensure a smooth process and avoid system congestion near the deadline," IRB said in a statement.

items.title: Tobacco control council calls for reintroduction of GEG provision in smoking law
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 29 (Bernama) -- The Malaysian Council for Tobacco Control (MCTC) has urged the government to consider reintroducing the Generational End Game (GEG) provision under the Control of Smoking Products for Public Health Act 2024 (Act 825). Its president, Prof Dr Murallitharan Munisamy, said the proposal was part of efforts to strengthen protection for future generations amid continuing public health challenges. He said there was room to consider additional measures such as the GEG policy to complement existing tobacco control initiatives.

items.title: 'Don't withdraw reports': Nancy urges abuse victims
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri has urged victims of domestic violence not to be afraid and not to withdraw their police reports. The Women, Family and Community Development minister expressed disappointment over those who retract their reports, noting that the ministry provides shelters and assistance to support victims. MORE TO COME © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Trump: Iran 'better get smart soon'
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump on Wednesday warned Iran to "better get smart soon" as efforts by Washington and Teheran to end hostilities appeared at a standstill. "Iran can't get their act together. They better get smart soon!"

items.title: Court hears RM19.5mil to Bersatu came with donation documents
items.summary: Norazeanti: Agreed. Norazeanti: Agreed. Norazeanti: Agreed.

items.title: Ministry wants list of domestic abusers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Women, Family and Community Development Ministry is seeking cooperation from relevant authorities to obtain a list of domestic violence offenders. Minister Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri said the move forms part of broader efforts to identify systemic weaknesses that adversely affect victims and to implement improvements. MORE TO COME © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Pahang MB wants return of three Malaysian elephants from Japan
items.summary: KUANTAN: Pahang Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Wan Rosdy Wan Ismail wants Zoo Taiping & Night Safari to take immediate action to bring back three Malaysian elephants – Dara, Amoi and Kelat – currently housed at Tennoji Zoo in Osaka, Japan. He said the elephants should be returned to Malaysia and placed at the Kuala Gandah National Elephant Conservation Centre in Temerloh, which he described as more suitable for their natural habitat and needs. Earlier, Arthur said that proposals from various parties, including NGOs, to bring back the three elephants should be given serious consideration .

items.title: Sabah rules out unlicensed crocodile hunting, cites legal limits
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Sabah cannot permit unlicensed crocodile hunting at present as it would contravene existing laws and could jeopardise ecosystem sustainability, says state Tourism, Culture and Environment Minister Datuk Jafry Ariffin. He said the crocodile species found in Sabah, Crocodylus porosus, was listed under Schedule 2, Part I, which classifies it as a protected animal under the Wildlife Conservation Enactment 1997. "Therefore, Sabah is unable to allow unlicensed crocodile hunting for now as it would contravene existing legal provisions and could jeopardise ecosystem sustainability.

items.title: Woman claims secret filming in JB hotpot restaurant toilet, police hunt suspect
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: A woman has lodged a police report after alleging she was secretly filmed while using a restroom at a hotpot restaurant along Jalan Trus here yesterday. The woman shared a screenshot of the police report on Facebook, claiming the incident occurred after midnight. Meanwhile, Johor Baru (South) police chief Assistant Commissioner Raub Selamat confirmed that a report had been lodged and that investigations are ongoing to identify the suspect.

items.title: Sabah moves to cushion tourism sector as Middle East conflict cuts airline routes
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 29 — Sabah’s tourism sector is bracing for turbulence as the ongoing Middle East conflict begins to ripple through global travel, prompting airlines to rationalise routes and forcing the state to rethink its connectivity strategy. State Tourism, Culture and Environment Minister Datuk Jafry Ariffin said airlines have cited profitability and operational concerns linked to the conflict as key reasons for the reduction of direct flights into Sabah, including routes to Kota Kinabalu and Tawau. These are among the reasons given for cutting back flights to Sabah,” he said during a question-and-answer session in the State Legislative Assembly.

items.title: Open burning triggers 1.6ha peat forest fire in Rantau Panjang
items.summary: RANTAU PANJANG: A peat forest fire covering 1.6 hectares broke out along Jalan Pasir Mas-Rantau Panjang today, believed to have been caused by open burning. Rantau Panjang Fire and Rescue Department operations chief Ahmad Nasaruddin Mahussain said seven personnel were dispatched to the scene after receiving an emergency call at 12.45pm. Thick smoke from the fire has reduced visibility in the area," he said.

items.title: Thunderstorms, heavy rain to hit KL, Putrajaya, eight states until 7pm
items.summary: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia), in a statement, said the weather was expected to affect all of Perlis, as well as several areas in Kedah, including Langkawi, Kubang Pasu, Pokok Sena, Padang Terap, Pendang, Kuala Muda, Sik, Baling and Kulim. In Selangor, the affected districts include Kuala Selangor, Klang, Gombak, Petaling, Kuala Langat and Sepang. In Sarawak, MetMalaysia issued warnings for Serian; Samarahan (Simunjan); Sri Aman (Sri Aman); Betong; Sarikei; Sibu (Sibu and Kanowit); Mukah (Tanjung Manis and Daro); and Kapit (Song).

items.title: Sabah faces critical shortage of 4,526 doctors, ratio far below national target
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 29 — Sabah is facing a critical shortage of doctors, with the state government saying the gap to the national average is a significant 4,526 doctors across the state. State Minister for Women, Family and Community Development Datuk Julita Majungki said that there are currently 3,431 government doctors and 1,397 private practitioners in Sabah, resulting in a doctor-to-population ratio of 1:775. “Based on our calculations, Sabah requires 9,354 doctors.

items.title: Samaiden completes solar PV project for Nippon Electric Glass, strengthens EPCC capabilities
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Renewable energy specialist Samaiden Group Bhd has completed a high-complexity solar photovoltaic (PV) installation for Nippon Electric Glass (Malaysia) Sdn Bhd (NEGM), reinforcing its engineering, procurement, construction and commissioning (EPCC) capabilities for large corporations. In a statement today, Samaiden said the project comprises rooftop solar PV and building-integrated photovoltaic (BIPV) systems, demonstrating a scalable and practical approach to renewable energy adoption. "The project represents an important step in the company's efforts to strengthen environmental responsibility while enhancing long-term energy efficiency," it said.

items.title: Proposal to bring home elephants Dara, Amoi, Kelat must be considered – Arthur
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Proposals by various parties, including non-governmental organisations (NGOs), to bring home three Malaysian elephants — Dara, Amoi and Kelat — should be given serious consideration, says Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Minister Datuk Seri Arthur Joseph Kurup. The three elephants were previously transferred to Tennoji Zoo in Osaka, Japan under a conservation collaboration, a move that has recently drawn public concern over their welfare. The transfer of the three elephants was agreed directly between ZTNS and Tennoji Zoo on a business-to-business (B2B) basis," he said.

items.title: Claims of Umno assemblymen rejoining state government false, says Jalaluddin
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Umno Negeri Sembilan chairman Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias has denied rumours that Umno state assemblymen have returned to hold positions in the Negri Sembilan state executive council (exco). Lenggeng assemblyman Datuk Asna Amin, who is also a former deputy speaker of the Negri Sembilan state legislative assembly, said Umno representatives in the state have not received any instructions from the party leadership. He said any further action would depend on directives from Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi.

items.title: Muhyiddin’s trial: Bank officer says Mamfor’s RM19.5m Bersatu donation ‘unusual’ as company sought several RM500,000 cheques on same day
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 29 — Construction company Mamfor Sdn Bhd’s donations totalling RM19.5 million to political party Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia in 2021 and 2022 were unusual because of the way the payments were made, the High Court heard today in Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin’s corruption and money laundering trial. Bank Muamalat’s PKNS Shah Alam assistant branch manager Norazeanti Miswan said it would not be unusual for an individual, company or institution to make political donations. But it was unusual in this case, as Bank Muamalat’s customer Mamfor had issued multiple cheques on the same day to the same recipient Bersatu, she said.

items.title: [UPDATED] Nine marriages, 11 children: Suspect in miscarriage assault was married 9 times, has 11 children
items.summary: Kedah police chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah said all of the suspect's marriages involved women who were civil servants in several states. He said preliminary investigations found the suspect currently has three wives, while six others were former wives. She also suffered a miscarriage," he said during a press conference at the Kedah police contingent headquarters here today (Wednesday).

items.title: MCMC's Nadi joins UN telecom agency's global digital network
items.summary: Government spokesman Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the cabinet was briefed on Nadi's achievement during its meeting today. "Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi also proposed that the rural community centres under the Rural and Regional Development Ministry streamline activities with Nadi in the near future," he said in his weekly post-cabinet meeting media briefing. On April 25, Fahmi said Nadi had officially been accepted as a member of the ITU Digital Transformation Centres network.

items.title: Rising star of France's far right weaponised a routine inquiry against public broadcasters
items.summary: A French fact-finding inquiry — normally a dull parliamentary ritual — has turned into a polarising political spectacle, giving France's far right an unexpected platform to wage a culture-war campaign against public broadcasters. As the six-month inquiry into France's 4-billion-euro-a-year public broadcasters draws to a close, media analysts and lawmakers say its impact will outlast the hearings themselves. RN leaders have relished turning the spotlight on public broadcasters that have long scrutinised the far right and its media allies.

items.title: Nephew and wife remanded over alleged RM15k jewellery robbery
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: An elderly couple were shocked to discover that the man believed to be their nephew robbed them of a gold bracelet worth about RM15,000 after breaking into their home last week. At first, the victim thought the suspect was someone else as he was wearing a mask. Earlier, a police team from the Special Investigation/Serious Crimes Division (D9) of the Batu Pahat district police headquarters launched an investigation and arrested both suspects at their home in Klang on Tuesday afternoon.

items.title: DPS, Xingfengwei to explore AGI data centre projects worth over RM4bil
items.summary: MELAKA: Melaka is set to strengthen its position as a leading regional digital hub as DPS Resources Bhd (DPS) explores the development of artificial general intelligence (AGI) data centre infrastructure projects here with an indicative value of about US$1.1 billion or over RM4 billion (US$1=RM3.95). DPS group chairman and founder Tan Sri Dr Sow Chin Chuan said the MoU represents an important step in DPS' strategy to unlock the long-term potential of its landbank and infrastructure capabilities in Melaka. "By collaborating with Xinfengwei, we aim to explore opportunities in AGI data centre infrastructure, where demand for high-performance computing capacity continues to grow globally," he said.

items.title: French teen in straw licking case allowed to leave Singapore
items.summary: SINGAPORE: A French teen facing criminal charges in Singapore for a straw licking stunt was granted permission on Wednesday to leave the country for three weeks on pledges to return. Didier Gaspard Owen Maximilien's clip went viral and triggered a backlash that led to his arrest in Singapore, which has a reputation for not tolerating bad behaviour. His next appearance in the Singapore court was also rescheduled from May 22 to May 29.

items.title: Short-term interbank rates end steady following Bank Negara operations
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Short-term interbank rates ended steady today following Bank Negara Malaysia's operations to absorb surplus liquidity from the financial system. Liquidity in the conventional system eased to RM40.84 billion from RM42.43 billion this morning, while liquidity in Islamic funds decreased to RM30.38 billion from RM32.51 billion. At 4pm, it called a RM40.80 billion conventional money market tender and a RM30.40 billion murabahah money market tender, both for one-day funds.

items.title: Fake account not ruled out in viral image insulting Selangor Sultan, says Fahmi
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Authorities have not ruled out the possibility that a fake account was used to upload a viral image allegedly edited to insult the Sultan of Selangor, says Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil. He said investigations were ongoing and further updates would be provided by the police and MCMC. Federal police Criminal Investigation Department director Datuk M. Kumar said investigations had been launched into a Facebook account owner.

items.title: Zurich units hit with penalities over sanction failures: Bank Negara
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bank Negara Malaysia has imposed administrative monetary penalties of RM1.04 million on Zurich General Insurance Malaysia Bhd (ZGIMB) and RM520,000 on Zurich General Takaful Malaysia Bhd (ZGTMB) for non-compliance with targeted financial sanction (TFS) requirements on Jan 19. In a statement today, the central bank said that, as reporting institutions (RIs), Zurich entities are required to update their sanctions database without delay upon publication of the Domestic List. RIs must also ascertain potential matches and make further inquiries to determine whether such matches are true matches.

items.title: Philippine VP faces impeachment vote in Congress after panel find probable cause
items.summary: MANILA: The Philippine House justice committee on Wednesday found probable cause to impeach Vice President Sara Duterte, moving a step closer to a process that could derail her bid to become the next president. In a vote of the committee, all 53 members agreed there was probable cause to impeach Duterte. Announcing her decision in February to run for the top job, Duterte apologised for helping Marcos to become president.

items.title: Finance Ministry issues guidelines to curb non‑essential spending, safeguard economy and public services
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 29 — The Ministry of Finance today confirmed that guidelines have been issued to ministries and agencies to reprioritise operating expenditure in response to global supply chain challenges and rising subsidy commitments. The ministry said the measure is part of a prudent fiscal management approach aimed at optimising government resources to ensure continued support for the people. It stressed that spending adjustments will be carried out without affecting critical public services or undermining economic stability.

items.title: US lawmakers to grill Pentagon chief Hegseth over Iran war
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth will face tough questions from lawmakers about the Iran war on Wednesday during his first testimony to Congress since the start of the conflict. Hegseth's appearance before the House Armed Services Committee – for a hearing on President Donald Trump's US$1.5 trillion defence budget request – comes with the war still unresolved and the economic fallout from it continuing around the globe. "Finally, Secretary Hegseth will come before the House Armed Services Committee this week.

items.title: Court orders govt to pay RM1mil over fatal fallen tree in Kuala Terengganu
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court has rejected the government's attempt to use the "act of God" defence in a lawsuit involving a fallen tree that killed one man and seriously injured two others in Kuala Terengganu. Judicial Commissioner Yusrin Faidz Yusoff ruled the Public Works Department (PWD) and the federal government were fully liable for the 2017 incident and ordered them to pay over RM1 million in damages. The victims and the estate of the deceased initiated legal action, naming the PWD, the government, and Roadcare (M) Sdn Bhd among the defendants, alleging negligence in the maintenance and upkeep of the roadside tree within the federal road reserve.

items.title: Finance Ministry: Expenditure cuts will not disrupt critical public services
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Expenditure adjustments by ministries and government agencies will be carried out without affecting critical public services or economic stability, the Finance Ministry said. In a statement today, the ministry said agencies have been directed to restructure their operating expenditure priorities in response to pressures from the global supply crisis and rising subsidy costs. Government spokesman Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said today that the move aligns with the government's plan to rationalise programme activities and implementation amid global supply challenges.

items.title: Iran takes seized ships to port, countries seek info on seafarers' safety
items.summary: LONDON: Two container ships seized by Iran near the Strait of Hormuz with about 40 crew aboard have been taken toward the port of Bandar Abbas, sources said on Thursday, Teheran having vowed to retaliate after US forces seized an Iranian vessel three days earlier. Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps seized the ships on Wednesday, one operated by MSC, the world's biggest container shipping group, and according to three sources, the other chartered by the firm. "Some 20 Iranians armed to the teeth stormed the ship.

items.title: Father pleads guilty to raping daughter; victim found 18 weeks pregnant
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: A 47-year-old labourer faces up to 30 years' imprisonment and a minimum of 10 strokes of the rotan after pleading guilty in the Magistrate's Court here today to raping his daughter, who was later found to be 18 weeks pregnant. The father of four entered the plea to two charges read before Magistrate Arun Noval Dass. According to the charges, the accused raped his 20-year-old daughter in his living room and bedroom here between March last year and January this year.

items.title: Lorry attendant jailed 18 years, caned over death of four-year-old adopted son
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, April 29 — A lorry attendant was sentenced to 18 years in prison and two strokes of the cane by the Sessions Court here today after pleading guilty to charges related to the death and injury of his adopted son. Judge Mohd Zul Zakiqudin Zulkifli sentenced Muhammad Firdaus Zamri, 31, to 18 years in prison for causing the death of four-year-old Muhammad Adamdanial Abdul Khafidh, at an unnumbered house in Kampung Banggol Katong, Terengganu, at about 6 pm on April 11. The charge was framed under Section 304 (a) of the Penal Code, which carries a prison sentence of up to 30 years.

items.title: Fahmi: MCMC working with Meta and police to investigate edited Sultan Selangor image case
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 29 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil today said investigations are underway into the circulation of an edited image of the Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah with authorities not ruling out the possibility that a fake account may have been used to upload the content. He said an individual has already provided a statement to the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC), denying responsibility for uploading material believed to contain 3R elements that allegedly insulted His Royal Highness the Sultan of Selangor. On Monday, the Selangor Council of the Royal Court strongly condemned a social media post that allegedly insulted the Sultan of Selangor, describing the act as irresponsible and potentially disruptive to public harmony.

items.title: MyJPJ app: Only MyDigital ID allowed for sign-in starting May 1
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 29 —The Road Transport Department (JPJ) will fully implement the Single Sign-On system for the MyJPJ Smart Application using MyDigital ID effective May 1, 2026. He said the new system will enable users to access a wide range of JPJ services through a secure, valid and officially recognised digital identity. "Users will only need MyDigital ID as their primary authentication method to access the MyJPJ application, reducing reliance on multiple login credentials while minimising data security risks,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Police nab trio over brutal hacking death of man in Tangkak
items.summary: The suspects, aged between 24 and 32, were detained at three separate locations last night following intelligence gathering and public tip-offs. District police chief Superintendent Roslan Mohd Talib confirmed the arrests. "We have detained three suspects.

items.title: Immigration arrests 77 undocumented foreign workers in eateries, packaging plant raids
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Seventy-seven foreign workers were detained in a two-day Immigration enforcement operation after they were found working without valid permits at eateries and a vegetable packaging facility here. Johor Immigration Department director Datuk Mohd Rusdi Mohd Darus said the operations were carried out following public tip-offs and weeks of intelligence, targeting premises employing undocumented foreign workers and those misusing social visit passes. The Immigration Department detained 26 Myanmar citizens, including six women, 11 Bangladeshi citizens, including one woman, and two Indonesian men, aged between 19 and 46.

items.title: Federal government to scale down events, Budget 2026 remains unchanged despite ongoing reviews
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 29 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the government has decided that all official events must be organised on a moderate scale, as instructed by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. On another development, Fahmi said the prime minister also emphasised the importance of coordinating the implementation of the Kampung Angkat Madani programme. He said the initiatives under the federal government must be closely coordinated.

items.title: Zahid moves to meet BN assemblymen amid Negeri Sembilan turmoil, Putrajaya stresses non‑interference
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 29 — Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has informed the Cabinet that he will be meeting Umno–Barisan Nasional (BN) state assembly members in Negeri Sembilan soon, amid political uncertainty in the state. Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said Zahid conveyed the plan during yesterday’s Cabinet meeting, following reports that 14 BN assemblymen had withdrawn their support for Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun, effectively signalling a loss of confidence in the state government. Fahmi added that Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim reaffirmed the Negeri Sembilan government continues to function as usual, stressing that the federal government will not interfere in matters relating to adat or customary affairs.

items.title: Government to enforce eKYC checks, ensuring children under 16 cannot hold social media accounts
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 29 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil today said the government, through the Ministry of Communications and the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) is continuing efforts to implement electronic Know Your Customer (eKYC) verification as part of tighter social media controls aimed at users under the age of 16. He said the primary objective of the measure is to ensure that children below 16 years old do not independently hold social media accounts. Fahmi added that the initiative is also intended to reduce the risk of children interacting with unknown adults online.

items.title: Oil supply stable for May–June, no disruptions expected for Petronas, says comms minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 29 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said crude oil supply remains stable in the immediate term, with no disruptions expected for May and June, particularly involving Petronas. He also said the meeting reviewed and continues to monitor global oil price movements, including crude oil and refined petroleum products such as RON95 and diesel. In light of these developments, Fahmi said the government is reviewing several subsidy-related measures and initiatives, including BUDI Madani RON95, BUDI Diesel and BUDI Individu.

items.title: Johor police confirm no criminal elements in shooting incident that injured ex-Bersatu leader Tan Lek Khang
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 29 — Johor police said they are ruling out criminal elements or the involvement of another party in the shooting incident involving former Johor Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia (Bersatu) associate chief Datuk Tan Lek Khang yesterday. Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said the 40-year-old politician was reportedly injured after his firearm accidentally discharged after training at the Ladang REM shooting range in Kota Tinggi yesterday. Ab Rahaman said the victim had a valid firearms license and permit for his guns.

items.title: Entrepreneur Aliff Syukri ordered to pay RM250,000 to former tourism minister Kadir Sheikh Fadzir
items.summary: Judge Roslan Mat Nor made the ruling after allowing the suit by the former Culture, Arts and Tourism Minister against Aliff Syukri. In addition to the damages, the court ordered Aliff Syukri to pay cost of RM50,000 to Abdul Kadir. Judge Roslan further ordered that all defamatory content concerning Abdul Kadir be removed from all of Aliff Syukri’s social media platforms within seven days.

items.title: Umno assemblymen in Negeri Sembilan dismiss rumours of U‑turn, await Zahid Hamidi’s instructions
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, April 29 — Negeri Sembilan Umno leaders have dismissed speculation that several of their assemblymen may return to support the state Pakatan Harapan government, insisting they will act only on instructions from party president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. The Star reported that state Umno chief Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias rejected claims that four assemblymen were preparing to reverse their decision and back Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun. “When the 14 of us made a decision to declare a loss of confidence in Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun’s leadership on Monday, it was made en bloc.

items.title: Diesel wholesaler fined RM130,000 for false invoices, withholding information
items.summary: BUTTERWORTH, 29 April — A company involved in retail fuel sales was fined a total of RM130,000 by the Sessions Court today on four counts of providing false information on invoices and refusing to submit information relating to its diesel purchases. Judge Nor Azah Kasran meted out the fine after the company, Profusion Petroleum Sdn Bhd, represented by its director, Lai Yong Keong, 47, pleaded guilty to all the charges. The false information was related to invoices or receipts for diesel purchases involving nine invoices.

items.title: Chief Statistician urges Malaysians to rebuild discipline, warning QR payments make saving harder than ever
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 29 — From stamp books and post office savings to instant QR payments, Malaysians’ relationship with money has shifted dramatically — and not necessarily for the better. That is the candid view of Chief Statistician Datuk Seri Mohd Uzir Mahidin, who said the convenience of digital payments may be quietly eroding the discipline that once defined how Malaysians saved. The underlying principle, he said, remains unchanged — saving is essentially about discipline.

items.title: Stats Dept warns Malaysians: Surplus years limited to ages 29–55, save wisely before income declines
items.summary: Chief Statistician Datuk Seri Mohd Uzir Mahidin said the data painted a stark but familiar picture of the country’s financial lifecycle — one where income only meaningfully exceeds spending between the ages of 29 and 55. The NTA framework tracks how labour income compares with consumption across a person’s life, revealing two prolonged deficit phases — at the beginning and end of life — sandwiching a relatively short surplus period in the middle. In the early years, from birth up to age 28, Malaysians operate in a “life cycle deficit”, where spending outpaces income.

items.title: Crackdown on illegal tourism turned up 71 cases of unlicensed operators in Sabah since last year
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 29 — Authorities have opened dozens of investigation papers against illegal tourism operators in Sabah since last year as part of an ongoing crackdown on unlicensed activities in the sector, the state assembly was told today. Assistant Tourism, Culture and Environment Minister Dr Andi Md Shamsureezal said a total of 47 investigation papers were opened in 2025, with compounds amounting to RM290,000. Dr Andi said enforcement against unlicensed tourism operators falls under the Tourism Industry Act 1992 (Act 482), which is under the jurisdiction of the federal Ministry of Tourism, Arts and Culture.

items.title: Selangor to encourage data centres to use raw or recycled water to not affect consumer water supply
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 29 — New data centre developments in Selangor are encouraged to use recycled water or treat raw water to suitable standards for cooling system needs. "Water demand from data centres is indeed very high. However, we must distinguish between water required for data centres and drinking water.

items.title: Man captured, put on seven day remand after being on the run for allegedly beating wife so severely she miscarried
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI, April 29 — A man accused of assaulting his wife so severely that she suffered a miscarriage in Ambangan Height last Thursday has been remanded for seven days to assist investigations. Police arrested the suspect yesterday in Alor Setar after he fled following the incident. At the time, Johor Bahru police said they were seeking a witness to assist investigations under Section 325 of the Penal Code.

items.title: Remand extended for army officer suspected of drunk driving in fatal MEX crash
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, April 29 — The remand period for the army officer suspected of driving under the influence in a fatal crash along the Maju Expressway (MEX) here has been extended until tomorrow. Kuala Lumpur Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said the extension was necessary to allow police to complete their investigation papers, according to Berita Harian . “The investigation papers are not yet complete and will be referred tomorrow morning for further instructions.

items.title: Man duped by Facebook investment scam promising returns of up to 750pc, loses RM610,000
items.summary: MUAR, April 29 — A businessman lost RM610,000 after falling victim to a non-existent investment scheme that promised unusually high returns. Muar district police chief ACP Raiz Mukhliz Azman Aziz said the 49-year-old man was drawn to an investment advertisement on Facebook before getting in touch with the suspect via WhatsApp. Raiz Mukhliz said the victim was also informed by the suspect that all investment matters would be handled by a broker.

items.title: Police probe RM415,000 Haj permit scam after Kuching man tricked by suspect in Mecca
items.summary: Kuching police chief ACP Alexson Naga Chabu said the victim was introduced to the Malay male suspect on October 30, 2024, at a restaurant near The Clock Towers in Mecca. The victim was offered assistance to obtain Haj permits and to invest in accommodation for pilgrims during the Haj and Umrah seasons. “Attracted by the offer, the victim made six online payment transactions to two different bank accounts.

items.title: Drop ‘negeri’ label, Jeffrey Kitingan insists, as Sabah deserves territory status
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 29 — Tambunan Assemblyman Datuk Seri Dr Jeffrey Kitingan called on the Government to drop the term “negeri” for Sabah, saying Sabah is a founding partner of Malaysia and not on the same level as the peninsular states. “The Sabah Legislative Assembly has already begun this shift, we now call it the Dewan Undangan Sabah, not Dewan Undangan Negeri and Sabah elections are now referred to as Pilihan Raya Umum Dewan Undangan Sabah, not Pilihan Raya Negeri,” he said. Sabah must stand as an equal to Sarawak and Malaya.

items.title: Cable theft suspects injured after 4WD crashes while fleeing police in Tuaran
items.summary: Tuaran Fire and Rescue Station (BBP) Chief Kurong Ak Beki said the department received a call about the incident at 4.44am. “Upon arrival, firefighters found the accident involved two vehicles — a Toyota Hilux Vigo carrying four victims (suspects) and an Isuzu Invader with one victim. Kurong said firefighters extricated the elderly man who was trapped in the Isuzu Invader using special equipment.

items.title: Three teenage boys arrested in Tawau over alleged sexual crimes against 14‑year‑old girl
items.summary: TAWAU, April 29 — Three teenage boys were arrested for alleged sexual crimes against a 14-year-old girl in several incidents since late last year. Tawau District Police Chief ACP Jasmin Hussin said two of the suspects, aged 14 and 17, were arrested in the Kg Jawa area at 11.50am on Monday by a team from the District Criminal Investigation Division (BSJD). “The arrests were made after police received a report last Saturday regarding allegations of sexual offences committed against the alleged victim at several separate locations around here between November 2025 and February this year,” he said.

items.title: Sabah assemblyman demands crackdown on foreigners fronting shops under locals’ names
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 29 — Usukan assemblyman Isnaraissah Munirah Majilis wants action taken on foreigners running businesses using Sabahans’ names and cited Pakistani-manned sundry shops statewide that are killing local businesses. It is an issue of local economic sovereignty,” she said when debating the Head of State Policy Speech, Tuesday. They are Pakistani,” she said, calling on the public to support only local-run businesses.

items.title: King of Malaysia meets Johor Menteri Besar at Istana Pasir Pelangi to discuss current issues
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 29 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, today granted an audience to Menteri Besar Johor Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi. During the meeting, His Majesty was briefed on matters relating to state development, the well-being of the people and current issues. According to a post on Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar’s official Facebook page, the audience session took place at Istana Pasir Pelangi, here.

items.title: James Chai back at MACC for second day of testimony in RM1.1b semiconductor probe
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 29 — A former aide to Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli, Chai Jin Shern or James Chai, is back at the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) headquarters here today to continue providing his statement in connection with an ongoing investigation. On the second day of the investigation, Chai arrived at 9.45 am to assist in the probe involving Arm Holdings Plc, a leading semiconductor company based in the United Kingdom. The anti-graft agency opened an investigation paper on Feb 16 into an agreement between the Malaysian government and a foreign company, reportedly valued at RM1.1 billion.
